text: The patient is positioned in a forty-five degree left up position for a laparoscopic, spleen-preserving distal pancreatectomy. The video shows port placements followed by entry into the lesser sac, exposing the pancreas. The surgeon divides the lower border of the pancreas and mobilizes it from the retroperitoneum, cirrhotic fascia, and mesocolon.

The surgeon carefully dissects the upper part of the pancreas, using repeated sharp and blunt dissection techniques to separate it from the splenic vessels, ensuring no injury occurs to these vessels.

The surgeon continues to mobilize the pancreas, revealing the tumor while carefully avoiding injury to the pancreas. Gentle layer-by-layer mobilization is performed, dividing the remaining attachments at the upper border to fully mobilize the distal pancreas. An endo-GIA stapler, size 60 with white cartridges, is positioned and fired slowly with graduated compression over two to three minutes to ensure hemostasis and minimize the risk of ductal leak.
The surgical specimen is removed using a retrieval bag. Gel foam is applied to the suture line, and a drain is positioned near the pancreatic remnant.
